primary_concern: Sepsis with acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL), anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL), and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests possible hepatic involvement. AKI progression is evident with stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) escalating to stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL), though the latter was excluded as an outlier. Platelet counts remain below 100 K/uL, indicating SOFA coagulation score ≥1.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
Creatinine elevation meets KDIGO stage 1 criteria (1.5× baseline) with progression to stage 3 excluded as outlier
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5× baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score threshold for coagulopathy
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements and hypotension status unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluid resuscitation

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Perform fluid resuscitation and reassess perfusion status
Order blood cultures and source control evaluation
Monitor for progression to septic shock (MAP ≥65, lactate trends)
Consider platelet transfusion if <50 K/uL or active bleeding
Implement AKI management bundle (diuresis, electrolyte monitoring)
doctor_handoff: Review for infection source localization, reassess AKI stage progression, and evaluate for vasopressor requirements. Confirm accuracy of excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) due to rapid 6.5 mg/dL rise.

disease_progression.observation: AKI stage 3 (Cr 8.9 excluded as outlier), platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
